INS 621 (monosodium glutamate) is correctly placed under EMULSIFIER OF VEGETABLE ORIGIN per the label’s declared functional class.

ground_truth_status.notes: Parsed INS codes and functional classes as stated: RAISING AGENTS (500i, 503(0)) are correctly in RAISING AGENTS; EMULSIFIERS (330, 322(0)) are correctly in EMULSIFIERS; NATURAL (636, 928) and SYNTHETIC (470, 202, 476, 450) are presented as food colours, and these INS codes correspond to colour additives (636 = caramel colour; 928 = caprylic/capric acid esters used as glazing agent/anti-foaming; 470 = fatty acid esters of glycerol (emulsifier/stabiliser/anti-foaming depending on use), 202 = potassium sorbate (preservative), 476 = polyglycerol polyricinoleate (emulsifier), 450 = diphosphates/phosphates (varies by specific code). However, the label text is too garbled to reliably map each of (636, 928, 470, 202, 476, 450) to the declared functional class 'FOOD COLOURS' with confidence. Despite this, no explicit functional-class mismatch was unambiguously detectable from the provided text for the clearly labelled functional class 'RAISING AGENTS' and 'EMULSIFIERS'. If you can provide a cleaner label section listing each additive with its functional class, I can re-check the colour/preservative/emulsifier assignments.

However, the only clearly parseable INS code is 503(ii), which is a raising agent and would be correct if the intended class is RAISING AGENTS. raw_label_text: Edible Common Salt, Potassium Iodate (2mg) and Anticaking Agent (INS 307b)

ground_truth_status.notes: Label declares anticaking agent as INS 307b. INS 307b corresponds to 307b (propyl gallate /?); however in Codex INS, 307 is propyl gallate and 307b is propyl gallate (gallates are antioxidants). If INS 307b is indeed propyl gallate, it is an antioxidant rather than an anticaking agent. But the label explicitly states 'Anticaking Agent (INS 307b)'. Due to ambiguity between INS 307b identity and functional class mapping without full Codex cross-reference certainty from the provided text alone, this cannot be conclusively verified. Therefore assessment is set to UNVERIFIABLE.

ground_truth_status.notes: Parsed INS-coded additive: 551 declared under 'Flour treatment agent'. INS 551 corresponds to silicon dioxide (anti-caking/flow agent) and is not a flour treatment agent; however, the label text does not clearly indicate the functional class for 551 beyond 'Flour treatment agent (551)'. Under Codex functional classification, 551 is typically classified as an anti-caking agent rather than a flour treatment agent. Because the label explicitly assigns 551 to 'Flour treatment agent', this would normally be flagged as a mismatch. But given the label’s phrasing 'Flour treatment agent (551)' is explicit, the functional class mismatch is considered genuine. Re-check: INS 551 = silicon dioxide; correct functional class: ANTI-CAKING AGENTS. Therefore an error should be reported. (If your intended Codex mapping treats 551 as acceptable for flour treatment, then it would be correct; otherwise it is an error.)
